summary: Checked downstream delta required to make the v0.57 PTO block encoding executable across benchmarks, TileOP API, LLVM, QEMU, tools/model, and GFSIM.
completion_rule: The downstream migration is complete only when tools/isa/check_pto_v057_downstream.py --strict reports no stale patterns and the benchmark flow passes with v0.57 descriptors and opcode ids.

targets.name: SuperNPUBench inline tile asm
required_delta: Emit v0.57 B.ITP source descriptors and B.OTA destination descriptors.
Replace old hardcoded TMA MGATHER/MSCATTER function ids with v0.57 TMA.Function ids.
Replace old hardcoded TEPL ids with v0.57 TEPL.TileOpcode ids from isa/v0.57/state/pto_encoding.json.
Use B.META for element-domain shape and valid-region metadata instead of encoding shape through C.B.DIMI-only tile shims.
completed_delta: benchmark-linxisa and benchmark-ptoisa template_asm.h emit B.ITP source descriptors and B.OTA destination descriptors.
Template allocation uses v0.57 B.OTA CellCountM1 and enforces at least one 128-byte CELL.
TLOAD and TSTORE templates use the v0.57 TMA.Function ids consumed by the LLVM and QEMU updates.

targets.name: TileOP Linx convenience API
required_delta: Expose the issue #14 TileOP API forms with signatures that match benchmark callsites.
Keep real PTO instruction forms such as TSTORE_FP, TINSERT, TCONCAT, and TFILLPAD assigned to concrete instruction families; expose TileOP wrappers when compiler lowering exists.
Map merged forms such as TSTORE/TSTORE_FP, TINSERT/TINSERT_FP, TCONCAT/TCONCATIDX, and the TFILLPAD family through form fields instead of new opcode ids.
Keep real TINSERT/TTRANS/TCONCAT/TFILLPAD lowering executable in the Linx API and GFSIM paths.
Do not add sync, communication, pipe-sync, or IR virtual forms as block-encoded TileOP instructions.
completed_delta: Linx TEPL convenience constants in workloads/pto_kernels/include/common/pto_tileop.hpp match the v0.57 TEPL.TileOpcode map and are checked by tools/isa/check_pto_v057_downstream.py.

common/pto_tileop.hpp exposes benchmark-facing v0.57 wrappers for TREMS, TCOLMAX, TCOLPROD, TCOLSUM, TROWPROD, TMOV, MGATHER, MSCATTER, TCVT, TDIVS, TEXP, TRECIP, TROWMAX, TROWSUM, TLOAD, TSTORE, TSTORE_FP, TEXPANDS, and TROWEXPAND.

TCONCAT and TCONCATIDX are exposed in workloads/pto_kernels/include/pto/common/pto_instr.hpp, implemented for CPU simulation in workloads/pto_kernels/include/pto/cpu/TConcat.hpp, and implemented for __LINXISA__ row-wise concat in workloads/pto_kernels/include/pto/linx/impl/PtoInstrImpl.hpp.
PTO-AS emits TCONCATIDX for pto.tconcatidx instead of overloading the plain TCONCAT call name.
PTO-AS no longer emits an instruction-looking scale-address helper for pto.tget_scale_addr; that op remains an IR/view operation with no LinxISA v0.57 EmitC instruction lowering.

required_patterns.expected_delta: TileOP API exposes TSTORE_FP as a form of TMA.Function.TSTORE

targets.name: LLVM Linx backend
required_delta: Teach MC asm parsing and printing to accept v0.57 B.ITP, B.OTA, and B.META descriptor syntax.
Lower TileOP/CUBE/TMA/FIXP operations using v0.57 opcode ids and merged form bits.
Change BSTART.TMA, BSTART.CUBE, BSTART.TEPL, and BSTART.FIXP local operation fields to the v0.57 8-bit opcode spaces.
Emit the v0.57 split tile descriptor surface.
completed_delta: MC parsing and printing accepts v0.57 B.ITP and B.OTA descriptor syntax with direct TileReg references T0 through T255, T0/TZERO source padding, reuse hints, last bits, source-pair ids, destination slots, and CellCountM1 destination allocation.
BSTART.TMA, BSTART.CUBE, BSTART.FIXP, and BSTART.TEPL MachineInstr operands carry 8-bit block-local function/selectors where required by v0.57.
Blockify emits B.ITP/B.OTA descriptor MachineInstrs for TLOAD, TSTORE, TMOV, CUBE TMATMUL, TEPL, VPAR local tile bases, and local scratch allocation.
FIXP TMOV lowering uses BSTART.FIXP Function=TMOV instead of an unmerged TMA TMOV selector.
CUBE TMATMUL and suffix forms lower through merged BSTART.CUBE Function=TMATMUL plus B.ARG form selection.
Combined tile descriptor parser, printer, MachineInstr, and opcode-table forms are removed from the LLVM target surface.
ninja -C compiler/llvm/build-linxisa-clang clang llvm-mc llvm-objdump -j10 passes after the descriptor split.

stale_patterns.expected_delta: BSTART.TMA function field must carry v0.57 TMA.Function u8 ids
BSTART.CUBE function field must carry v0.57 CUBE.Function u8 ids
BSTART.TEPL tile opcode field must carry v0.57 TEPL.TileOpcode u8 ids

targets.name: QEMU Linx target
required_delta: Decode v0.57 B.ITP and B.OTA descriptors with 8-bit TileReg ids.
Update tile execution dispatch to v0.57 TEPL, CUBE, TMA, and FIXP opcode spaces.
Decode merged CUBE and TMA/FIXP forms through B.ARG/form fields instead of distinct old function ids.
Remove old masks that truncate CUBE/TMA/FIXP to 5-bit local functions and TEPL to 10-bit TileOp fields.
completed_delta: BSTART.TMA, BSTART.CUBE, BSTART.FIXP, and BSTART.TEPL translation begins direct v0.57 tile headers instead of remapping packed PAR-era TileOp values.
TMA dispatch uses v0.57 ids: MGATHER=0, MSCATTER=1, TLOAD=2, TPREFETCH=3, and TSTORE/TSTORE_FP=4; the removed TCVT compatibility slot is no longer decoded as TMA.
CUBE dispatch uses merged v0.57 ids: TGEMV=0 and TMATMUL=1; old accumulate/conversion selector rows are removed from active dispatch.
QEMU decodes B.ITP and B.OTA directly, with split source-pair and destination-allocation descriptor state and 8-bit TileReg ids.
TLOAD consumes B.OTA CellCountM1 allocation; TSTORE consumes B.ITP sources and obtains source size from tracked TileReg allocation metadata.
Vector local tile bases now derive TA/TB/TC/TD from B.ITP and TO/TS from B.OTA.
qemu-system-linx64 builds successfully with ninja -C emulator/qemu/build-linx qemu-system-linx64 after the descriptor split.

stale_patterns.expected_delta: separate source and destination tile descriptor state
v0.57 block-local opcode fields are u8
v0.57 TEPL.TileOpcode is a u8 local opcode id
dispatch on full v0.57 u8 TMA/CUBE/FIXP ids
dispatch on full v0.57 u8 TEPL ids
TCVT is TEPL.TileOpcode.TCVT, not a TMA compatibility id
CUBE conversion/accumulate suffixes are B.ARG forms of merged CUBE rows

targets.name: tools/model ISA executor
required_delta: Recognize v0.57 BSTART.FIXP and BSTART.TSTORE as tile/data block starts.
Regenerate generated ISA tables after v0.57 descriptor and opcode changes.
Remove unmerged generated aliases for TMATMUL suffixes, TMOV, and TSTORE when those forms are selected by v0.57 opcode family plus B.ARG.

stale_patterns.expected_delta: TMOV is FIXP.Function.TMOV with form selection
TSTORE/TSTORE_FP share TMA.Function.TSTORE with form selection

completed_delta: generated_tables.cpp exposes B.ITP and B.OTA descriptor rows.
Generated BSTART table labels use merged v0.57 selector surfaces: BSTART.TMA, BSTART.CUBE, and BSTART.FIXP Function/DataType forms.
Unmerged generated aliases for BSTART.TMOV, BSTART.TMATMUL.ACC, and BSTART.TSTORE are removed from the table surface.

targets.name: GFSIM BlockISA model
required_delta: Decode v0.57 B.ITP and B.OTA descriptors with 8-bit TileReg ids.
Replace old TEPL mode/function splitting with direct v0.57 TEPL.TileOpcode dispatch.
Update TMA/CUBE/FIXP function dispatch to merged v0.57 opcode families and B.ARG form selection.
Maintain FIXP execution paths for TCONCAT, TFILLPAD, TINSERT, TTRANS, TMOV, and related real PTO instruction families.
completed_delta: TMA function dispatch uses v0.57 ids: MGATHER=0, MSCATTER=1, TLOAD=2, TPREFETCH=3, and TSTORE/TSTORE_FP=4.
CUBE function dispatch uses merged v0.57 ids: TGEMV=0 and TMATMUL=1; suffix variants are no longer separate BSTART function ids.
TEPL decode detects the v0.57 BSTART.TEPL match pattern and dispatches directly from the TEPL.TileOpcode byte.
FIXP decode recognizes TDEQUANT, TEXTRACT, TINSERT, TMOV, TCONCAT, TFILLPAD, TQUANT, and TTRANS as real FIXP.Function ids.
The combined block-argument size calculator is removed because v0.57 source descriptors no longer encode allocation size.
FIXP TMOV reuses the existing tile-move execution path; TPREFETCH is treated as a no-op hint in the TMA engine.
GFSIM block type metadata includes FIXP and cmake --build build --target gfsim -j10 passes in model/LinxCoreModel.
